text:
Market today: Mood is cautiously constructive - semis and tech holding near highs, small-caps reportedly having a strong year which lifts risk appetite broadly. The real driver for your names is macro/geopolitical noise (Iran strikes, AI policy headlines) keeping volatility elevated. That matters for your leveraged and margin positions.

Your holdings: SOXL (-7.06%) is the one to watch - it's a 3x leveraged semi ETF, decay is real, and your stop at $200 is ~15% below current price. On margin that's a painful gap if semis roll. QQQI (-0.78%) is fine as an income anchor but watch the $52 stop. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with limited data - tiny sizing only, margin amplifies any air-pocket drop hard.

Watch: (1) Whether SMH holds current levels - it's your canary for SOXL. (2) The White House AI crackdown headline - any follow-through policy action could hit your entire tech-heavy book fast.

Bottom line: Your leveraged names (SOXL especially) are the margin risk - respect those stops and don't add until the AI policy picture clears.
plays: SOXL: Stop at $200 is wide for a 3x ETF on margin - consider tightening it manually toward $215-220 to cut real dollar risk if semis stall.
SPCX: Keep sizing tiny and manage manually - no new adds until price history and liquidity develop; IPO volatility plus margin is a dangerous combo.
No new buys today - CRWD scanner setup (entry 741-742, stop 740) has a razor-thin stop buffer under current price; risk/reward doesn't justify adding on margin right now.
